records.ety: [F. annuler, LL. annullare, annulare, fr. L. ad to + nullus none, nullum, neut., nothing. See Null, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Annulled (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Annulling.]

defs: To reduce to nothing; to obliterate.
quotes: Light, the prime work of God, to me's extinct. And all her various objects of delight Annulled.

defs: To make void or of no effect; to nullify; to abolish; to do away with; -- used appropriately of laws, decrees, edicts, decisions of courts, or other established rules, permanent usages, and the like, which are made void by component authority.
quotes: Do they mean to annul laws of inestimable value to our liberties?
